info.name: Webex BroadWorks
info.description: The Webex BroadWorks Calling APIs provide access to advanced calling features and user management for BroadWorks-powered Webex Calling deployments. These APIs support provisioning of users and devices, call control, feature management, device inventory, and detailed reporting. Service providers and enterprises can automate onboarding, integrate with OSS/BSS systems, manage user entitlements, and monitor call quality. The APIs are designed for scalable, multi-tenant environments and support seamless integration with existing telephony infrastructure.

request.description:
Provision a new BroadWorks workspace for Cisco Webex services.

This API allows a Service Provider to provision a workspace for an existing customer.

response.name: OK
Bad Request: The request was invalid or cannot be otherwise served. An accompanying error message will explain further.
Unauthorized: Authentication credentials were missing or incorrect.
Forbidden: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.
Not Found: The URI requested is invalid or the resource requested, such as a user, does not exist. Also returned when the requested format is not supported by the requested method.
Method Not Allowed: The request was made to a resource using an HTTP request method that is not supported.
Conflict: The request could not be processed because it conflicts with some established rule of the system. For example, a person may not be added to a room more than once.
Gone: The requested resource is no longer available.
Unsupported Media Type: The request was made to a resource without specifying a media type or used a media type that is not supported.
Locked: The requested resource is temporarily unavailable. A Retry-After header may be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before attempting the request again.
Precondition Required: File(s) cannot be scanned for malware and need to be force downloaded.

Too Many Requests: Too many requests have been sent in a given amount of time and the request has been rate limited. A Retry-After header should be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before a successful request can be made.

Service Unavailable: Server is overloaded with requests. Try again later.
Gateway Timeout: An upstream server failed to respond on time. If your query uses max parameter, please try to reduce it.

request.description:
Generate a monthly reconciliation report.
Partners who have moved to extended wholesale subscription model should generate reports via [Wholesale Billing Reports](/docs/api/v1/wholesale-billing).

item.name: Update Directory Sync for a BroadWorks Enterprise

variable.description: (Required) A unique identifier for the enterprise in question.

request.description: This API lets a Partner Admin enable or disable directory sync for the customer's Broadworks enterprise on Webex.

query.description: The user ID of the subscriber on BroadWorks.
The Person ID of the Webex subscriber.
The email address of the subscriber.
The Provisioning ID associated with this subscriber.
The Service Provider supplied unique identifier for the subscriber's enterprise.
Only include subscribers with a provisioning status change after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
The provisioning status of the subscriber. This Parameter supports multiple comma separated values. For example : status=error,provisioned,provisioning.
Only include subscribers created after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
Indicates if the subscriber was self activated, rather than provisioned via these APIs.
Limit the maximum number of subscribers returned in the search response, up to 100 per page. Refer to the [Pagination](/docs/basics#pagination) section of [Webex REST API Basics](/docs/basics).

request.description: This API lets a Service Provider search for their associated subscribers. There are a number of filter options that can be combined in a single request.

response.name: OK

query.description: The user ID of the subscriber on BroadWorks.

The Provisioning ID associated with this subscriber.
The Service Provider supplied unique identifier for the subscriber's enterprise.
Only include subscribers with a provisioning status change after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
The provisioning status of the subscriber. This Parameter supports multiple comma separated values. For example : status=error,provisioned,provisioning.
Only include subscribers created after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
Indicates if the subscriber was self activated, rather than provisioned via these APIs.
Limit the maximum number of subscribers returned in the search response, up to 100 per page. Refer to the [Pagination](/docs/basics#pagination) section of [Webex REST API Basics](/docs/basics).

The Provisioning ID associated with this subscriber.
The Service Provider supplied unique identifier for the subscriber's enterprise.
Only include subscribers with a provisioning status change after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
The provisioning status of the subscriber. This Parameter supports multiple comma separated values. For example : status=error,provisioned,provisioning.
Only include subscribers created after this date and time. Epoch time (in milliseconds) preferred, but ISO 8601 date format also accepted.
Indicates if the subscriber was self activated, rather than provisioned via these APIs.
Limit the maximum number of subscribers returned in the search response, up to 100 per page. Refer to the [Pagination](/docs/basics#pagination) section of [Webex REST API Basics](/docs/basics).

request.description:
Provision a new BroadWorks subscriber for Webex services.

This API lets a Service Provider map a BroadWorks subscriber to a new or existing Webex user and assign the required licenses and entitlements for Webex and Meetings.

response.name: OK
Bad Request: The request was invalid or cannot be otherwise served. An accompanying error message will explain further.
Unauthorized: Authentication credentials were missing or incorrect.
Forbidden: The request is understood, but it has been refused or access is not allowed.
Not Found: The URI requested is invalid or the resource requested, such as a user, does not exist. Also returned when the requested format is not supported by the requested method.
Method Not Allowed: The request was made to a resource using an HTTP request method that is not supported.
Conflict: The request could not be processed because it conflicts with some established rule of the system. For example, a person may not be added to a room more than once.
Gone: The requested resource is no longer available.
Unsupported Media Type: The request was made to a resource without specifying a media type or used a media type that is not supported.
Locked: The requested resource is temporarily unavailable. A Retry-After header may be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before attempting the request again.
Precondition Required: File(s) cannot be scanned for malware and need to be force downloaded.

Too Many Requests: Too many requests have been sent in a given amount of time and the request has been rate limited. A Retry-After header should be present that specifies how many seconds you need to wait before a successful request can be made.
Internal Server Error: Something went wrong on the server. If the issue persists, feel free to contact the [Webex Developer Support team](/explore/support).
Bad Gateway: The server received an invalid response from an upstream server while processing the request. Try again later.
Service Unavailable: Server is overloaded with requests. Try again later.
Gateway Timeout: An upstream server failed to respond on time. If your query uses max parameter, please try to reduce it.

variable.description: (Required) A unique identifier for the subscriber in question.

request.description:
This API lets a Service Provider update certain details of a provisioned BroadWorks subscriber on Webex.
The updated items will not be immediately reflected in the response body, but can be subsequently obtained via the [Get a BroadWorks Subscriber](/docs/api/v1/broadworks-subscribers/get-a-broadworks-subscriber) API once the status has transitioned from the updating state to the provisioned state.

request.description:
Verify the likely success of provisioning a broadworks subscriber.
The Prerequisite for using this API is to have at least one Broadworks Cluster configured against partner using Partner Hub. The Provisioning Precheck APIs supports two variants of Broadworks Provisioning Precheck Requests. Please refer to [Using the Provisioning Precheck API](/docs/api/guides/webex-for-broadworks-developers-guide#using-the-precheck-provisioning-api) section in [Webex for BroadWorks](/docs/api/guides/webex-for-broadworks-developers-guide) guide for more information.

item.name: Send Consent User Move Email to Pending Broadworks Subscribers

variable.description: (Required) A unique identifier for the subscriber in question.

request.description: Allows a Service Provider to send the user consent move email to a subscriber who is currently in `pending_user_migration` state.
